question: [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower who calls repayAndWithdrawCollateral with a valid partial repayment and a collateralWithdrawals list be permanently blocked from completing the atomic repay+withdraw when a second activated collateral's IOracle.price() begins reverting, under the precondition that the borrower has debt > 0, collateralBitmap has at least two bits set, and the oracle for the non-withdrawn collateral reverts, trigger the call sequence repayAndWithdrawCollateral -> Midnight.repay (succeeds, debt reduced) -> Midnight.withdrawCollateral -> isHealthy -> IOracle.price() (reverts) -> entire tx reverts, violating the invariant that a valid repayment followed by a health-preserving withdrawal must succeed if the borrower is solvent, causing scoped impact: valid borrower's withdrawal reverts unexpectedly even though repayment would leave position healthy? Proof idea: fuzz collateralIndex, assets, units across LLTV tiers {0.385e18..1e18} with two activated collaterals where oracle[1] is a RevertingOracle and assert repayAndWithdrawCollateral reverts despite Midnight.repay alone succeeding.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged lender who calls buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with a valid collateralWithdrawals list be permanently DoS'd from withdrawing their own collateral in the same bundle when any activated collateral oracle for the taker reverts, under the precondition that the taker has debt > 0, collateralBitmap has a bit set for a collateral whose oracle reverts, and the takes succeed via try/catch, trigger the call sequence buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral -> take (succeeds, filledUnits == targetUnits) -> withdrawCollateral -> isHealthy -> IOracle.price() (reverts) -> entire tx reverts, violating the invariant that a successful take followed by a health-preserving collateral withdrawal must not revert due to an unrelated oracle, causing scoped impact: valid lender's collateral withdrawal reverts unexpectedly? Proof idea: unit test with RevertingOracle on collateral index != collateralWithdrawals[0].collateralIndex and assert the bundle reverts while direct Midnight.withdrawCollateral on the same index would also revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged borrower who calls supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget be blocked from borrowing when a second activated collateral's oracle reverts, under the precondition that the borrower already has debt > 0 with collateralBitmap having a bit set for a reverting oracle, and the new take would increase sellerDebtIncrease > 0, trigger the call sequence supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget -> supplyCollateral (succeeds) -> take -> isHealthy (called at line 476 for seller) -> IOracle.price() (reverts) -> take reverts -> filledUnits < targetUnits -> OutOfOffers revert, violating the invariant that a borrower with sufficient new collateral must be able to borrow, causing scoped impact: valid borrower's borrow via bundle reverts unexpectedly? Proof idea: fuzz units, collateralIndex with two activated collaterals where oracle[1] is RevertingOracle and assert supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged borrower who calls supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget be blocked from borrowing when a second activated collateral's oracle reverts, under the precondition that the borrower already has debt > 0 with collateralBitmap having a bit set for a reverting oracle, trigger the call sequence supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget -> supplyCollateral (succeeds) -> take -> isHealthy (seller health check) -> IOracle.price() (reverts) -> take reverts -> filledSellerAssets < targetFilledSellerAssets -> OutOfOffers revert, violating the invariant that a borrower with sufficient collateral must be able to borrow, causing scoped impact: valid borrower's borrow via bundle reverts unexpectedly? Proof idea: fuzz targetSellerAssets, maxUnits with RevertingOracle on non-primary collateral and assert bundle reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged lender who calls buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral be blocked from withdrawing collateral when a non-withdrawn activated collateral's oracle reverts, under the precondition that the taker has debt > 0 and collateralBitmap has a bit set for a reverting oracle at index != collateralWithdrawals[i].collateralIndex, trigger the call sequence buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral -> take (succeeds) -> withdrawCollateral -> isHealthy -> IOracle.price() (reverts) -> entire tx reverts, violating the invariant that a valid collateral withdrawal must not be blocked by an unrelated oracle, causing scoped impact: valid lender's collateral withdrawal reverts unexpectedly? Proof idea: unit test with two activated collaterals, RevertingOracle on index 1, withdrawal on index 0, assert bundle reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral / Midnight.isHealthy] Can a valid borrower become liquidatable due to a single activated collateral's oracle returning 0, under the precondition that the borrower has N >= 2 activated collaterals, oracle[k].price() returns 0 for some k, and the remaining collaterals' maxDebt contributions are insufficient to cover debt, trigger the call sequence isHealthy -> collateralBitmap loop -> IOracle(collateralParam.oracle).price() returns 0 -> maxDebt += collateral[k].mulDivDown(0, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(lltv, WAD) = 0 -> maxDebt < debt -> isHealthy returns false, violating the invariant that a borrower whose true collateral value covers debt must not be liquidatable, causing scoped impact: valid borrower becomes liquidatable due to oracle returning 0? Proof idea: fuzz collateral amounts and LLTV values across all tiers, set oracle[k].price() = 0, assert isHealthy returns false and liquidate succeeds.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / isHealthy] Can a valid borrower with a single collateral become liquidatable when the oracle returns a price just below the health threshold due to mulDivDown rounding, under the precondition that collateral * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * lltv / WAD rounds down to debt - 1 while the true value is >= debt, trigger the call sequence liquidate -> bitmap loop -> maxDebt = collateral.mulDivDown(price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(lltv, WAD) = debt - 1 -> originalDebt > maxDebt -> NotLiquidatable not triggered, violating the invariant that healthy positions must not be liquidatable due to rounding, causing scoped impact: valid borrower becomes liquidatable due to mulDivDown rounding at health boundary? Proof idea: fuzz collateral in [1, 2^128-1], price in [1, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE], lltv in allowed tiers, find (collateral, price, lltv) where mulDivDown(mulDivDown(collateral, price, 1e36), lltv, 1e18) == debt - 1 but true value >= debt.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate] Can a liquidation revert unexpectedly when the oracle for the liquidated collateral returns 0 and repaidUnits > 0, under the precondition that collateralIndex is in the borrower's bitmap, oracle[collateralIndex].price() returns 0, and the liquidator passes repaidUnits > 0 with seizedAssets = 0, trigger the call sequence liquidate -> bitmap loop -> liquidatedCollatPrice = 0 -> seizedAssets = repaidUnits.mulDivDown(lif, WAD).mulDivDown(ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, 0) -> division by zero -> revert, violating the invariant that an unhealthy position must always be liquidatable, causing scoped impact: liquidation of unhealthy position reverts unexpectedly when oracle returns 0? Proof idea: unit test with oracle.setPrice(0), call liquidate(collateralIndex, 0, repaidUnits=1, ...) and assert revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate] Can a liquidation revert unexpectedly when any activated collateral oracle reverts, even if the liquidated collateral's oracle is healthy, under the precondition that the borrower has N >= 2 activated collaterals, oracle[k].price() reverts for some k != collateralIndex, trigger the call sequence liquidate -> bitmap loop -> IOracle(market.collateralParams[k].oracle).price() reverts -> entire liquidate reverts, violating the invariant that an unhealthy position must always be liquidatable, causing scoped impact: liquidation of unhealthy position reverts unexpectedly due to unrelated oracle? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.withdrawCollateral / isHealthy] Can a valid borrower's collateral withdrawal revert unexpectedly when a non-withdrawn activated collateral's oracle reverts, under the precondition that the borrower has N >= 2 activated collaterals, oracle[k].price() reverts for some k != collateralIndex, and the borrower has debt > 0, trigger the call sequence withdrawCollateral -> clearBit(collateralIndex) -> isHealthy -> bitmap loop -> IOracle(market.collateralParams[k].oracle).price() reverts -> isHealthy reverts -> withdrawCollateral reverts, violating the invariant that a valid collateral withdrawal must not be blocked by an unrelated oracle, causing scoped impact: valid borrower's withdrawal reverts unexpectedly? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.take / isHealthy] Can a valid seller's take revert unexpectedly when an activated collateral oracle reverts, under the precondition that the seller has debt > 0, collateralBitmap has a bit set for a reverting oracle, and liquidationLocked(id, seller) is false, trigger the call sequence take -> tExchange(LIQUIDATION_LOCK_SLOT, id, seller, true) -> callbacks -> tExchange(LIQUIDATION_LOCK_SLOT, id, seller, false) -> isHealthy(offer.market, id, seller) -> IOracle.price() reverts -> take reverts, violating the invariant that a healthy seller must be able to take offers, causing scoped impact: valid borrower's take reverts unexpectedly due to oracle revert? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.isHealthy] Can a borrower with a single collateral appear unhealthy when oracle.price() returns a value in [1, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE - 1] that causes mulDivDown(collateral, price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE) to round down to 0, under the precondition that collateral < ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE / price (i.e., collateral * price < ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE), trigger the call sequence isHealthy -> maxDebt += collateral.mulDivDown(price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(lltv, WAD) = 0 -> maxDebt = 0 < debt -> isHealthy returns false, violating the invariant that a borrower with non-zero collateral and non-zero oracle price must not be treated as having zero collateral value, causing scoped impact: valid borrower with dust collateral becomes liquidatable? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / isHealthy] Can a multi-collateral borrower's maxDebt be systematically underestimated due to independent mulDivDown rounding per collateral, under the precondition that the borrower has N activated collaterals each contributing a fractional unit of maxDebt that rounds down, trigger the call sequence isHealthy -> for each i: maxDebt += collateral[i].mulDivDown(price[i], ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(lltv[i], WAD) -> sum of N rounded-down values < true sum -> maxDebt < debt -> isHealthy returns false, violating the invariant that a borrower whose true weighted collateral value >= debt must be healthy, causing scoped impact: valid multi-collateral borrower becomes liquidatable due to accumulated rounding error? Proof idea: fuzz N in [2, 16], collateral[i] and price[i] values near rounding boundaries, assert sum(mulDivDown(mulDivDown(c_i, p_i, 1e36), lltv_i, 1e18)) < true_sum for some inputs.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate] Can an invalid borrower stay healthy when a high-value oracle price for one collateral compensates for undercollateralization in another, under the precondition that the borrower has two activated collaterals, oracle[0].price() returns a very large value making collateral[0].mulDivDown(price[0], ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(lltv[0], WAD) >> debt, trigger the call sequence liquidate -> maxDebt = sum of contributions -> maxDebt >= originalDebt -> NotLiquidatable() revert, violating the invariant that a borrower whose collateral[1] alone is insufficient to cover debt must be liquidatable, causing scoped impact: invalid borrower stays healthy due to oracle price cross-collateral compensation? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate] Can a borrower self-poison their liquidation by activating a collateral with a reverting oracle, under the precondition that the borrower calls supplyCollateral with a collateralIndex whose oracle reverts (oracle is not called in supplyCollateral), then becomes unhealthy, trigger the call sequence supplyCollateral (oracle not called, succeeds) -> oracle starts reverting -> borrower becomes unhealthy -> liquidate -> bitmap loop -> IOracle(market.collateralParams[k].oracle).price() reverts -> liquidate reverts, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must always be liquidatable, causing scoped impact: liquidation of unhealthy position reverts unexpectedly due to self-poisoned oracle? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate] Can a liquidator be forced to use only the seizedAssets input (not repaidUnits) when the liquidated collateral's oracle returns 0, under the precondition that oracle[collateralIndex].price() = 0 and the borrower is unhealthy due to other collaterals, trigger the call sequence liquidate -> liquidatedCollatPrice = 0 -> seizedAssets > 0 path: repaidUnits = seizedAssets.mulDivUp(0, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivUp(WAD, lif) = 0 -> liquidator seizes collateral for 0 repaid -> _position.collateral[collateralIndex] -= seizedAssets -> underflow revert (since collateral[collateralIndex] = 0 by bitmap invariant), violating the invariant that liquidation must not revert for unhealthy positions, causing scoped impact: liquidation reverts unexpectedly when oracle returns 0 and seizedAssets > 0? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / RCF check] Can a liquidation revert unexpectedly due to the RCF check using liquidatedCollatPrice when the oracle price is very low, under the precondition that the borrower is unhealthy, oracle[collateralIndex].price() is very small (near 1), and repaidUnits > maxRepaid, trigger the call sequence liquidate -> liquidatedCollatPrice = very_small -> maxRepaid = (debt - maxDebt).mulDivUp(WAD*WAD, WAD*WAD - lif*lltv) -> RCF check: collateral.mulDivDown(liquidatedCollatPrice, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(WAD, lif).zeroFloorSub(maxRepaid) < rcfThreshold -> if rcfThreshold = 0 and the expression > 0, RecoveryCloseFactorConditionsViolated revert, violating the invariant that a liquidator must be able to liquidate an unhealthy position, causing scoped impact: liquidation reverts unexpectedly due to RCF interaction with low oracle price? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / post-maturity LIF] Can a post-maturity liquidation revert unexpectedly when the oracle for the liquidated collateral returns 0 and repaidUnits > 0, under the precondition that block.timestamp > market.maturity, oracle[collateralIndex].price() = 0, repaidUnits > 0, trigger the call sequence liquidate -> postMaturityMode = true -> lif = min(maxLif, WAD + ...) -> seizedAssets = repaidUnits.mulDivDown(lif, WAD).mulDivDown(ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, 0) -> division by zero -> revert, violating the invariant that post-maturity positions must always be liquidatable, causing scoped impact: post-maturity liquidation reverts unexpectedly due to oracle returning 0?
